original_hypothesis.title: CauldronV4 reduceSupply Can Drain MIM Without Accounting for Outstanding Fee Obligations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: CauldronV4.reduceSupply() allows the masterContract owner to withdraw up to the entire MIM balance from the cauldron's BentoBox account, without checking if some of that balance is needed to cover accrued feesEarned. If reduceSupply drains all available MIM, subsequent withdrawFees() calls will revert because the BentoBox transfer will fail due to insufficient balance, permanently locking protocol fees.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: reduceSupply calculates maxAmount from the full BentoBox balance with no reservation for feesEarned. The feesEarned represents protocol revenue that should be transferable via withdrawFees(), but reduceSupply can drain the backing.

proof_of_signal.pattern: reduceSupply uses maxAmount = bentoBox.toAmount(MIM, bentoBox.balanceOf(MIM, this), false) — no deduction for accrueInfo.feesEarned

preconditions: MasterContract owner calls reduceSupply for maximum amount
No outstanding borrows providing future repayment to cover fees

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: reduceSupply can withdraw ALL MIM; feesEarned tracks accrued interest + opening fees; withdrawFees tries to transfer feesEarned from same balance
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Malicious or careless masterContract owner calls reduceSupply(type(uint256).max) draining fee reserves
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol fees become permanently locked (DoS on fee withdrawal)

economic_questions: How much fee revenue is typically accumulated before withdrawal?
Can borrowers still repay after reduceSupply drains all MIM?
Is there an operational procedure to prevent this?
validation_requirements: Verify reduceSupply + withdrawFees interaction when balance < feesEarned
Test scenario: accrue fees, reduceSupply max, then withdrawFees
Check if existing borrows provide sufficient incoming repayments

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Admin extracts MIM meant for fee recipients

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Typical feesEarned, MIM balance, borrow utilization
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: reduceSupply(maxAmount) followed by withdrawFees() revert scenario

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
